person.headline: Founder & CTO at Shape | Building AI co-workers and custom AI products
person.about: Žiga Kerec is the engineering co-founder and CTO of Shape, a Slovenian AI venture studio with offices in Berlin, New York, and Ljubljana. He owns all engineering decisions for the studio's own SaaS products (ProductAI, Wondercut, MomentClip, MapleNorth) and its client delivery. A full-stack developer by training, he previously built Cherrydeck (a location-based photographer discovery platform) from the ground up and held a Tech Lead role at Relum, an AI-driven content startup. He is an active builder and writer on LinkedIn covering agentic coding, AI product development, and engineering team management.

education.school: Faculty of Computer and Information Science (FRI), University of Ljubljana
education.degree: Bachelor's Degree, Computer and Information Sciences

past_experience.title: Tech Lead
past_experience.note: AI-driven content creation platform; listed on Crunchbase with Žiga as Tech Lead alongside co-founder Aljoša Zidan

past_experience.title: Co-Founder
past_experience.note: Livestream shopping marketplace; one of three founders who pivoted Shape from agency to product studio

past_experience.title: Full-Stack Software Engineer
past_experience.note: Location-based talent discovery app for Instagram photographers. Helped design and build it from scratch; worked on PostgreSQL, SailsJS/Node.js backend, ReactJS + MobX frontend. Onboarded thousands of users in months.

recent_activity_themes: Agentic coding and AI-native software development
Shipping AI MVPs in 6-week fixed-scope engagements
Internal product growth: ProductAI at 1,000+ paying users, Wondercut feature iteration
Claude Code vs Cursor tooling comparisons

company.name: Shape (Shape Labs / Shape d.o.o.)
company.summary: Shape is an AI-native venture studio that both builds and ships its own SaaS products (ProductAI, Wondercut, MomentClip, MapleNorth) and takes on client engagements for AI automation and MVP development. Its key differentiator is that it battle-tests every technique on its own P&L before selling it to clients. Engagements run as fixed-scope MVP builds (from $48K, 6 weeks) or dedicated monthly pods ($35–60K/month). Co-founders: Marko Balažic (product), Aljoša Zidan (commercial/software), Žiga Kerec (engineering/CTO).
company.industry: AI Venture Studio / AI Software Development
company.hq_location: Ljubljana, Slovenia (also Berlin, Germany and New York, USA)
company.employee_count_estimate: 3 named co-founders; boutique team, likely <15 total (listed in Product Design C
products: ProductAI — AI studio photos for ecommerce (background removal, scene generation, brand-consistent shots)
Wondercut — AI video extractor surfacing shareable moments from long footage; 1,000+ paying users within 8 months of launch
MomentClip — AI video highlights tool for creators and brand teams
MapleNorth (2026) — AI operations co-pilot for short-term rental (STR) owners: guest comms, cleaning ops, dynamic pricing
Knjigovid — AI invoice reader that categorises and exports to accounting software
Poliglot — RAG-based bulk translation tool (built for a named client)
Wondercut (brand experience) — On-site AI activations turning visitors into portrait leads at events/retail
company.positioning: AI-native process automation studio; positions against generic dev shops and pure consultancies by proving its methods survive production on its own products first. Tagline: 'We don't bolt AI onto products for the pitch deck. We build it into the core.'

funding.total_raised_estimate: No external funding identified; appears bootstrapped / self-funded from services revenue and own-product ARR

hiring_signals.note: No open roles found on shape-labs.com careers page or job boards attributable to this specific Shape d.o.o. entity. Studio is explicitly described as a small three-founder team. No hiring signals detected.

recent_news.title: MapleNorth Launched — AI Operations Co-Pilot for Short-Term Rental Owners
recent_news.why_it_matters: New revenue line in the fast-growing STR tech market; signals continued product-studio expansion beyond creative AI tools.

recent_news.title: Shape publishes prolific SEO content blitz on agentic AI development (10+ articles in May 2026)
recent_news.why_it_matters: Aggressive top-of-funnel content strategy targeting 'agentic AI development', 'AI MVP services' keywords — signals active lead-gen investment and growth intent.

recent_news.title: ProductAI reaches 1,000+ paying users within 8 months of launch
recent_news.why_it_matters: Validates the studio's self-funded product model and demonstrates a proven ecommerce AI use case with traction. Strong social proof for client pitches.

recent_news.title: Shape delivers full-stack AI MVP for Owesa (professional networking & job marketplace) — verified Clutch review
recent_news.why_it_matters: Demonstrates cross-sector delivery capability including agentic features, social feeds, and automated marketing agents — expands perceived TAM.

recent_news.title: Wondercut EU project opens at Bled Castle; Shape expands physical AI experience activations across Europe
recent_news.why_it_matters: Shows offline-to-online AI experience capability, differentiated from pure software shops — possible expansion into experiential/retail segments.

competitors.name: Bonsai (formerly Bench, or comparable AI dev studios like AI Fund / Andrew Ng's studio)
competitors.note: AI Fund (Andrew Ng) is a well-funded AI venture studio model; backed by $370M+, operates at much larger scale and with formal VC co-investment. Competes for the 'AI venture studio' positioning globally.
competitors.vs_positioning: Shape wins on speed (6-week MVP vs. multi-month), cost efficiency (European rates, $48K floor), and proof-by-own-products. AI Fund targets deep-tech and enterprise; Shape targets seed founders and SMBs needing fast AI delivery.

competitors.name: Povio
competitors.note: Slovenian product design and development company building digital products across AI, cloud, and development. Well-known in the Ljubljana tech scene; sponsor of local AI/developer meetups.
competitors.vs_positioning: Shape positions as an operator-studio (ships its own products) vs. Povio's pure-service model. Shape's fixed-scope MVP at known price points and self-proven AI tooling is a sharper offer for founders wanting speed.

competitors.name: Shaper Labs
competitors.note: Fixed-price MVP development shop with similar 4–8 week timelines. Competes directly on the 'fast fixed-price MVP' positioning and targets the same early-stage founder audience.
competitors.vs_positioning: Shape differentiates via its own live AI products (ProductAI, Wondercut) as proof points, agentic coding discipline, and a venture-studio co-build option with equity. Shaper Labs is services-only.

competitors.name: Lemon.io / Toptal (AI dev talent marketplaces)
competitors.note: Developer talent marketplaces often compared by founders evaluating Shape. Offer on-demand engineers but no product ownership, no eval discipline, no own-product proof.
competitors.vs_positioning: Shape's dedicated pod model ($35–60K/month) competes at similar spend but includes strategy, AI eval infrastructure, and operator accountability that marketplaces cannot offer.

competitors.name: Forum Ventures AI Studio
competitors.note: B2B AI venture studio providing $250K capital + co-building support. Competes for equity-deal founders who want both money and build support.
competitors.vs_positioning: Shape's equity co-build model (no disclosed capital injection) is more accessible for non-US founders; Shape also takes on pure fee-based client work, giving it dual revenue streams Forum Ventures lacks.

domestic_competitors.name: Povio
domestic_competitors.note: Largest known Slovenian digital product studio; builds across AI, cloud, and mobile. Has US presence but HQ and engineering team are Ljubljana-based. Active in local AI developer community.
domestic_competitors.vs_positioning: Shape wins on AI-native depth (own AI products in production), boutique speed, and venture-studio co-build terms. Povio is larger-team, more process-heavy.

domestic_competitors.name: Celtra
domestic_competitors.note: Slovenian creative automation platform using AI for ad and content production. Overlaps with Shape's ProductAI and Bentoads in the AI creative automation space. Enterprise-focused.
domestic_competitors.vs_positioning: Celtra targets large enterprise brands with a SaaS platform; Shape targets founders and SMBs with bespoke AI builds and its own lightweight products. Different buyer profile, but same underlying AI creative automation category.

domestic_competitors.name: ABC Accelerator (portfolio companies)
domestic_competitors.note: Slovenia's leading startup accelerator; some portfolio companies are AI dev studios or competing in adjacent venture-building. Not a direct competitor but shapes the same founder pipeline Shape targets for co-builds.
domestic_competitors.vs_positioning: Shape offers equity co-builds without the accelerator cohort structure; faster to production, no batch model. Founders who want to build fast and keep more equity may prefer Shape's model.

commercials.web_traffic_estimate: No third-party traffic estimate retrieved for shape-labs.com specifically. Site publishes 20+ SEO articles targeting high-volume AI development keywords (agentic AI, AI MVP, etc.) — organic traffic li
commercials.aov_estimate: ~$50,000–$72,000 per client engagement (fixed-scope MVP); ~$40,000–$60,000/month for retainer pods. Own-product SaaS AOV not publicly available.
commercials.pricing_model: Dual model: (1) Client services — Fixed-Scope MVP from $48K / 6 weeks; Dedicated Pod $35–60K/month for 3–12 months; mini
typical_price_points: Fixed-Scope MVP: from $48K, 6 weeks (most engagements $48K–$72K all-in)
Client project range observed: $12,000 (small MVP) to $160,000 (long-term engagement)

outreach.role_summary: Žiga is the engineering decision-maker and co-founder of a 3-person AI venture studio that ships both its own AI SaaS products and client MVPs. As CTO, he controls all tech stack choices, tooling, infrastructure, integrations, and developer hiring. He is an operator-builder who personally codes, use
likely_pain_points: Scaling AI evaluation pipelines and keeping eval infrastructure robust as multiple products run simultaneously (ProductAI, Wondercut, MomentClip, MapleNorth)
Managing compute and LLM API costs across a portfolio of AI SaaS products, especially as paying user counts grow (ProductAI at 1,000+ users)
Maintaining development velocity and code quality across a very small team handling both client work and own products — context-switching tax is high
Client acquisition and pipeline predictability: the studio has a strong product story but relies heavily on content SEO and word-of-mouth — outbound is thin
Tooling coherence: using Claude Code and Cursor interchangeably suggests unresolved workflow orchestration; a routing or orchestration layer could save significant developer time
hooks: You wrote about choosing between Claude Code and Cursor depending on the task — we help teams like Shape build a router so you stop making that choice manually and just get the right output.

ProductAI hitting 1,000+ paying users in 8 months is a strong signal — at that scale, eval infrastructure and LLM cost management become the bottleneck. We help AI SaaS teams instrument that layer without slowing down shipping.

You're running both a client studio ($48K MVPs) and four live products simultaneously with three people — the hardest problem at that stage is context-switching and shared infra. We've helped studios in the same position build shared eval + deployment scaffolding that keeps both

The article on agentic coding vs vibe coding shows you think hard about where AI accelerates vs. where it cuts corners — that's the right mental model. We help CTO-founders like you audit the specific gaps between what agentic coding delivers today and what your team actually nee

Wondercut just launched in 2026 and MapleNorth is new — managing two fresh launches while serving client pods is a capacity crunch. Tools that reduce your own infrastructure overhead (monitoring, eval, deployment) buy back engineering hours without adding headcount.
talking_points: Shape's own-product proof model ($48K MVP → ProductAI at 1,000+ paying users) is a strong differentiator in the market; any tool or service you bring in should reinforce, not complicate, that story
The Dedicated Pod model ($35–60K/month) implies recurring revenue but also recurring delivery pressure — tooling that reduces context-switch cost pays back quickly at that cadence
MapleNorth and MomentClip are both 2026 launches; Žiga is likely in the most resource-constrained phase of the studio's history right now
The studio is bootstrapped (no external funding detected) — ROI and payback speed will be more persuasive than feature lists; lead with cost savings or hours recovered
Žiga personally writes about AI engineering — he responds to peer-level technical conversations, not vendor pitches. Lead with insight, not a demo request
outreach.tone_match: Peer-level, direct, technically grounded. Žiga writes in a blunt, no-BS register on LinkedIn (e.g. 'boring is fast'). Avoid corporate language, long intros, and feature-list pitches. Open with a specific observation from his public writing
icebreakers: Žiga — saw your post on firing a talented-but-unreachable developer and replacing him with someone average. That framing really resonated. It's the same tradeoff studios hit with AI tooling: brilliant but unpredictable vs. reliable and shippable. Happy to share what we've seen work for small teams running multiple AI p

Žiga, the Claude Code vs Cursor piece you wrote is one of the cleaner breakdowns I've seen of the two modes. We work with a few studios running similar dual-track setups and have noticed a pattern around where the routing decision should actually live — curious if you'd find it useful to compare notes.

Saw that ProductAI crossed 1,000 paying users recently — congrats. At that milestone the LLM cost curve usually starts to bite. We help AI SaaS teams model and optimise that before it becomes a surprise invoice.

meta.research_notes: Web traffic figures for shape-labs.com could not be retrieved from SimilarWeb or Semrush snippets — monthly_traffic left as 0 with explanation in aov_estimate. Hiring page for shape-labs.com returned no relevant results (queries surfaced unrelated Shape entities). Shape's funding status is unconfirmed as bootstrapped — no Crunchbase funding rounds listed and no press releases found; this is the most likely scenario for a 3-person studio, but cannot be ruled out with certainty. The $200K pre-seed mentioned in the Pablu article appears to refer to Pablu specifically, not Shape's current operation. Žiga's exact start date at Shape is inferred (~2018) from '7 years of shipping ventures' phrasing on the site as of 2025.
